titles.title: Athlytics: A Reproducible Framework for Endurance Data Analysis

institution.institution_name: Joint Laboratory of Biomedical Artificial Intelligence, Shanghai East Hospital, Tongji University, Shanghai, China

citation.article_title: The training–injury prevention paradox: Should athletes be training smarter and harder?
citation.author: Gabbett
citation.unstructured_citation: Gabbett, T. J. (2016). The training–injury prevention paradox: Should athletes be training smarter and harder? British Journal of Sports Medicine, 50(5), 273–280. https://doi.org/10.1136/bjsports-2015-095788

citation.article_title: Acute:chronic workload ratio: Conceptual issues and fundamental pitfalls
citation.author: Impellizzeri
citation.journal_title: International Journal of Sports Physiology and Performance
citation.unstructured_citation: Impellizzeri, F. M., Tenan, M. S., Kempton, T., Novak, A., & Coutts, A. J. (2020). Acute:chronic workload ratio: Conceptual issues and fundamental pitfalls. International Journal of Sports Physiology and Performance, 15(6), 907–913. https://doi.org/10.1123/ijspp.2019-0864

citation.article_title: What role do chronic workloads play in the acute to chronic workload ratio? Time to dismiss ACWR and its underlying theory
citation.author: Impellizzeri
citation.unstructured_citation: Impellizzeri, F. M., Woodcock, S., Coutts, A. J., Fanchini, M., McCall, A., & Vigotsky, A. D. (2021). What role do chronic workloads play in the acute to chronic workload ratio? Time to dismiss ACWR and its underlying theory. Sports Medicine, 51(3), 581–592. https://doi.org/10.1007/s40279-020-01378-6

citation.article_title: The jackknife and the bootstrap for general stationary observations
citation.author: Künsch
